summary: On March 5-6, 2026, the "Rubicon" Center reported 128 and 107 targets destroyed respectively, including Ukrainian 2S22 "Bogdan" and M109 self-propelled artillery, T-64BV tanks, and personnel across the Donbas, Sumy, and Krasny Liman directions, with operators also conducting FPV air defense against Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs. In Krasny Liman, a FAB-3000 bomb with a universal gliding module struck Ukrainian fortified positions, captured via the "KVN" fiber-optic FPV drone. Ukrainian forces used FPV drones launched from USVs, controlled via Starlink, to strike a Russian Ka-27 helicopter and the "Sivash" drilling platform in the Black Sea. On the Donetsk front, a Ukrainian "Furia" reconnaissance drone was shot down before transmitting data, and a "Lyutyi" strike was prevented on the Sumy front. Discussions highlighted the growing use of the "Yolka" interceptor drone, the need to expand the kill zone deeper into enemy territory, and the adoption of Chinese PTS-303 rotating antenna systems for FPV drone countermeasures.
